perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this MNDA without cause upon five (5) days written notice given to the other, provided that confidentiality obligations under Section A of this Attachment A shall survive termination hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this MNDA without cause upon five (5) days written notice given to the other. Confidentiality obligations under Section A of this Attachment A shall terminate immediately upon notice of termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the survival of confidentiality obligations upon termination. Originally, these obligations survive termination. Now, they terminate immediately, creating uncertainty and potential disputes. This contradicts the original intent of protecting confidential information even after the agreement ends.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may terminate this MNDA without cause upon five (5) days written notice given to the other. Confidentiality obligations under Section A of this Attachment A shall terminate immediately upon notice of termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that confidentiality obligations survive termination, while the changed text specifies that these obligations terminate immediately upon notice of termination. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, reiterating that confidentiality obligations terminate immediately. This direct conflict regarding the survival of confidentiality obligations constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof. This MNDA shall not be modified or changed in any manner except in a writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof. This MNDA may be modified or changed in any manner with only verbal agreement by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states modifications must be in writing and signed. The modified text allows verbal agreements for modifications, creating uncertainty about how changes are legally made. This contradicts the principle of documented agreement modifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof. This MNDA may be modified or changed in any manner with only verbal agreement by both parties. If a court of competent jurisdiction finds any of the provisions hereto so over-broad as to be unenforceable, such provisions may be reduced in scope by the court to the extent it deems necessary to render the provision reasonable and enforceable.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that modifications to the agreement must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text modifies this to allow verbal agreements for modifications. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which creates a direct conflict within the document regarding how modifications to the agreement can be made. The original text and change text show the original text was changed to something that created an in-text contradiction. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Consistent with other provisions herein, each party assures that it will not knowingly, without obtaining prior authorization from the U.S. Dept. of Commerce Office of Export Administration, transmit directly or indirectly the technical data received pursuant hereto or the immediate product (including processes and services) produced directly by use of such technical data to Afghanistan, People's Republic of China, or any other Country Group Q, S, W, T, or Z country specified in Supplement No. I to Part 370 of U.S. Dept. of Commerce Export Administration Regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding any other provisions herein, each party has full discretion to transmit directly or indirectly the technical data received pursuant hereto or the immediate product (including processes and services) produced directly by use of such technical data to any country, without the need for authorization from the U.S. Dept. of Commerce Office of Export Administration.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the export control clause. It removes the restriction on exporting technical data to specified countries, potentially violating U.S. export laws. It creates a direct conflict with the intent and compliance requirements outlined in the original provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding any other provisions herein, each party has full discretion to transmit directly or indirectly the technical data received pursuant hereto or the immediate product (including processes and services) produced directly by use of such technical data to any country, without the need for authorization from the U.S. Dept. of Commerce Office of Export Administration.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that each party has full discretion to transmit technical data to any country without authorization, which directly contradicts the original text's restriction on exporting technical data to specified countries. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, highlighting that the change itself introduces a direct conflict with the intent and compliance requirements outlined in the original provision and defined as inconsistencies in the definitions.

perturbation.original_text: No rights or obligations other than expressly recited herein are to be implied here from.
perturbation.changed_text: Implied rights and obligations, derived from common industry practices and reasonable interpretations of this agreement, are fully enforceable, in addition to the express terms outlined herein.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction by directly opposing the original clause that limits rights and obligations to only those expressly stated. It creates uncertainty by suggesting implied rights and obligations can be enforced, potentially altering the scope of contractual obligations. The new clause would introduce ambiguity over what the rights and obligation of each parties are.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Implied rights and obligations, derived from common industry practices and reasonable interpretations of this agreement, are fully enforceable, in addition to the express terms outlined herein. Nothing except that expressly stated herein shall affect either party's present or prospective rights under any country's patent laws, or be construed as granting any license under any present or future patent or application therefor, or preclude marketing any product unless such marketing constitutes unauthorized disclosure of INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibited any implied rights or obligations. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that implied rights and obligations are fully enforceable. This directly opposes the initial restriction, creating a clear inconsistency within the contract. The contradicted text then includes this same contradictory language.

perturbation.original_text: This MNDA shall be construed, interpreted and applied in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts.
perturbation.changed_text: This MNDA shall be construed, interpreted and applied in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, irrespective of any conflict-of-law principles.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction by changing the governing law of the contract from Massachusetts to Delaware. This creates uncertainty and potential legal disputes, since this creates a conflict regarding which state's laws will be used to interpret the contract. Both clauses appear in the same section and thus creating the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This MNDA shall be construed, interpreted and applied in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, irrespective of any conflict-of-law principles.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the MNDA is governed by the laws of Massachusetts. The changed text alters this to Delaware. The contradicted text also states that the MNDA is governed by the laws of Delaware. The change introduces a contradiction by changing the governing law from Massachusetts to Delaware. Since both clauses are in the same section, it creates a direct conflict regarding which state's laws will be used to interpret the contract, falling under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: If a court of competent jurisdiction finds any of the provisions hereto so over-broad as to be unenforceable, such provisions may be reduced in scope by the court to the extent it deems necessary to render the provision reasonable and enforceable.
perturbation.changed_text: If a court of competent jurisdiction finds any of the provisions hereto so over-broad as to be unenforceable, such provisions shall be automatically null and void, with no opportunity for judicial modification or reformation.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates a contradiction by altering the recourse if a provision is deemed unenforceable. The original text allows the court to reduce the scope, whereas the new text states that the provision becomes void. This introduces uncertainty about the enforceability of provisions deemed too broad.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof. This MNDA may be modified or changed in any manner with only verbal agreement by both parties. If a court of competent jurisdiction finds any of the provisions hereto so over-broad as to be unenforceable, such provisions shall be automatically null and void, with no opportunity for judicial modification or reformation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that if a provision is found over-broad, it becomes automatically null and void with no opportunity for judicial modification. The contradicted text repeats the changed text word for word, but the key issue is that the original text allowed for judicial modification, creating a direct contradiction with the new version, and therefore a contradiction exists since it breaks compatibility with the original condition.
